seoTitle: Dandanning vs Dangin — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Dandanning and Dangin. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Dandanning and Dangin.
heading: Dandanning vs Dangin — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Dandanning, Western Australia and Dangin, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Dangin has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Dandanning (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Dandanning or Dangin safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Dandanning has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Dangin scores 27/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Dandanning scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Dangin scores 26/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Dandanning or Dangin?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Dangin has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.6% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
